subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: Given \(A \in \mathbb{C}^{m \times n}\) with \(m \geq n\), show that \(A^*A\) is nonsingular if and only if \(A\) has full rank.

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: If \(u,v \in \mathbb{C}^m\) and \(A=uv^*\), show that \(\lVert A\rVert_2=\lVert u\rVert_2\lVert v\rVert_2\).

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: Show that the eigenvalues of \(S\) are pure imaginary.

subparts.label: (c)
subparts.text: Show that the matrix \(Q=(I-S)^{-1}(I+S)\) is unitary.

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: Define the least squares solution to \(Ax=b\).

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Derive the normal equations for the least squares problem.

subparts.label: (c)
subparts.text: Prove that the unique solution to the least squares problem is \((A^T A)^{-1}A^T b\).

subparts.label: (d)
subparts.text: Describe how to solve the least squares problem using the SVD decomposition of \(A\).

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: Prove that every square matrix \(A\) has a Schur factorization.

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: If \(A\) is normal (so \(A^*A=AA^*\)) show that the triangular matrix in its Schur factorization is diagonal.

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: If \(A\) has a collection of \(m\) linearly independent eigenvectors, show that \(A\) is diagonalizable.

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: When \(A\) is diagonalizable, prove the Cayley–Hamilton theorem for \(A\), i.e. \(A\) satisfies its own characteristic polynomial.
